analysis: CPTAC-BRCA ERBB2 protein vs HER2 status — decoupling support

source.proteomics: cBioPortal brca_cptac_2020_protein_quantification (CPTAC-BRCA mass-spec, Krug 2020 Cell / Mertins 2016 Nature; datatype LOG2-VALUE, log2 ratio vs reference so negatives = below-reference)
source.her2_labels: cBioPortal brca_cptac_2020 native clinical attributes (ERBB2_UPDATED_CLINICAL_STATUS, ERBB2_GENE_AMPLIFIED); native patientIds, no cross-ID join

he_mil_reference.attribution: sjpark modeling eval (agents/modeling); quoted here, NOT recomputed in this workspace
he_mil_reference.pairing: cohort-level juxtaposition — NOT patient-paired to the proteomics subset

note: Confirms known biology (amplification->overexpression): HER2+ tumors have strongly elevated ERBB2 protein. This is a re-confirmation of established fact, NOT a new finding. Its role is to establish that HER2 is molecularly REAL at the protein level in this cohort, so the low H&E-MIL AUROC reflects morphological silence, not a label/DNA artifact.
